text.title: Backend Testing Across Languages Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the testing-backend-languages methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Rails tests MUST use RSpec + factory_bot. let is lazy; let! forces immediate evaluation in before blocks — pick one per case explicitly. Slow I/O tests MUST be tagged :integration.
rule.rationale: RSpec + factory_bot is the community standard; let/let! distinction prevents subtle ordering bugs; :integration tag lets CI filter fast/slow tiers.

rule.statement: Laravel tests MUST use Pest function syntax + RefreshDatabase for DB-touching tests. HTTP endpoint tests MUST use $this->postJson/getJson + assertJson chains.
rule.rationale: Pest is the Laravel default; RefreshDatabase wraps each test in a transaction to prevent state leak; JSON helpers check status + structure in one call.

rule.statement: Spring Boot tests MUST default to slices: @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) for services, @DataJpaTest for repositories, @WebMvcTest for controllers. @SpringBootTest is allowed only for explicit @Tag("integration") tests.
rule.rationale: @SpringBootTest adds 5-30 seconds of context startup per class; slices boot only the relevant layers and run 10x faster.

rule.statement: .NET tests MUST use xUnit ([Fact]/[Theory]) + Moq + FluentAssertions. WebApplicationFactory is allowed only for HTTP integration scenarios.
rule.rationale: xUnit is the .NET standard; FluentAssertions surfaces clear error messages; WebApplicationFactory boots full ASP.NET — reserve for integration.

rule.statement: Rust async tests MUST use #[tokio::test] (or runtime-specific equivalent). Mixing #[test] with an async fn is forbidden.
rule.rationale: #[test] over async fn compiles but the future is never polled; assertions never run; the test 'passes' vacuously.

rule.statement: Mocks MUST be shallow: only direct collaborators of the SUT are mocked. Deep mock graphs (>2 levels) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Deep mocks freeze refactors and turn green tests into liars; prefer pure functions + thin integration tests.

rule.statement: The SUT MUST NOT be invoked inside the assertion expression. Always capture the result in a variable first.
rule.rationale: assert service.do() == service.do() runs the SUT twice, doubling side-effects; refactor-resistant tests capture once and assert against the captured value.
